questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A magnet freely suspended in a vibration magnetometer makes 40 oscillations per minute at place $A$ and 20 oscillations per minute at a place $B$. If the horizontal component of earth's magnetic field at $A$ is $36 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~T}$, then its value at $B$ is
questions.solution:
Time period of magnet, $T=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{I}{M H}}$
In both the condition magnet is same.
So,
$\begin{aligned} T & \propto \frac{1}{\sqrt{H}} \\ \frac{T_A}{T_s} & =\frac{\sqrt{H_n}}{H_A}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{gathered}\frac{60 / 40}{60 / 20}=\sqrt{\frac{H_n}{36 \times 10^{-4}}} \\ \frac{1}{2}=\frac{\sqrt{H_n}}{36 \times 10^{-6}}\end{gathered}$
Squaring both sides
$\frac{1}{4}=\frac{H_n}{36 \times 10^{-6}} \Rightarrow H_n=9 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~T}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A magnet of length $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ and magnetic moment $1 \mathrm{Am}^2$ is placed along side $A B$ of an equilateral triangle $A B C$. If the length of the side $A B$ is $10 \mathrm{~cm}$. The magnetic induction at the point $C$ is $\left(\mu_0=4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{Hm}^{-1}\right)$
questions.solution:
$l=10 \mathrm{~cm}, M=1 \mathrm{Am}^2$
Magnetic induction at point ${ }^{+} \mathrm{C}$ (In equatorial position), $\begin{aligned} B & =\frac{\mu_a}{4 \pi} \frac{M}{(C O)^2}=10^{-7} \times \frac{1}{\left(\sqrt{10^2-5^2}\right)^3} \\ & =\frac{10^{-7} \times 1}{(\sqrt{75})^3}=\frac{10^{-7}}{\left(5 \sqrt{3} \times 10^{-2}\right)^3} \\ & =\frac{10^{-7}}{125 \times 3 \sqrt{3} \times 10^{-6}}=\frac{10^{-1}}{375 \times 1.732} \\ & =0.000154=1.54 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~T} \\ & =10^{-4} \mathrm{~T}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

therefore force between them ∝ x - 4

questions.q: The ratio of magnetic fields due to a bar magnet at the two axial points P<sub>1</sub> and P<sub>2</sub> which are separated from each other by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn></math>. P<sub>1 </sub>Point is situated at<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> from the centre of the magnet. (Points P<sub>1</sub> and P<sub>2 </sub>are on the same side of magnet and distance of P<sub>1</sub> and P<sub>2 </sub>from the centre of magnet is much greater than the distance of two ends of the magnet from the centre). Magnetic length of the bar magnet is
options: 5 cm
10 cm
15 cm
20 cm
questions.solution:
B 1 B 2 = 25 2
μ 0 4π M d 1 ( d 1 2 − l 2 ) 2 μ 0 4π M d 2 ( d 2 2 − l 2 ) 2 = 25 2
d 1 d 2 × ( d 2 2 − l 2 ) 2 ( d 1 2 − l 2 ) 2 = 25 2
d 1 =10 cm, d 2 =20 cm
10 20 × ( 20 2 − l 2 10 2 − l 2 ) 2 = 25 2
400− l 2 =5( 100− l 2 )
4 l 2 =100⇒ l 2 =25
l=5 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A magnetic needle free to rotate in a horizontal plane is placed at the centre of a circular current-carrying coil whose axis is perpendicular to the magnetic meridian at that place. It is also known that the magnetic declination at this place is zero and in this condition, the magnetic needle is pointing towards the north-west. Now, if we reverse the direction of current in the coil, then the magnetic needle will
questions.solution: If there is no current in the coil, then the needle would point north but due to the magnetic field of the coil, it is pointing north-west. This means the magnetic field due to the coil is towards west and it is equal to the horizontal component of earth's magnetic field. So when we reverse the direction of current the magnetic field due to the coil will be towards east and the magnetic needle will point north-east.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

The magnetic induction at a point midway between them is

magnetic field is a vector quantity. at point p two magnetic field are acting at 90o

questions.q: When a ferromagnetic substance is heated above its Curie temperature, then :
questions.solution: If a ferromagnetic substance is heated above its Curie temperature. It looses some (not all) the magnetism properties  materials and becomes paramagnetic. Curie temperature of Iron is 770oc. i.e if iron is heated above 770oc then it behave like paramagnetic substance.

questions.q: The magnetization of bar magnet of length 5 cm, cross sectional area 2 <math><mi>c</mi><msup><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> and net magnetic moment <math><mn>1 </mn><mi>A</mi><msup><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> is
questions.solution:
Given, l=5cm=5×10-2m,
a=2cm2=2×10-4m2
And M=1Am-2
The magnetisation of the bar magnet is given by
l=MV
where, V = volume of bar magnet = a×l
⇒l=Ma×l
Substituting given values, we get
l=12×10-4×5×10-2=110-5
=1×105Am

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two small bar magnets of length <math> <mrow> <mtext>1 cm</mtext> </mrow> </math> each have magnetic moments <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1.20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>A </mi><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.00</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> respectively. They are placed on a horizontal table parallel to each other with their <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtext mathvariant="italic">N</mtext></math> poles pointing towards the south. They also have the same magnetic equator and separation between them is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo>.0</mo><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. What will be the value of the resultant horizontal magnetic induction at the mid-point <math> <mrow> <mtext>O</mtext> </mrow> </math> of the line joining their centres? (Horizontal component of earth's magnetic induction is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.6</mo><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi>Wb</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> )
questions.solution:
Bnet=B1+B2+BH

=μ04πM1x3+μ04πM2x3+BH

= { 10 −7 ( 1.2+1 ) } ( 0.1 ) 3 +3.6× 10 −5

=2.56×10-4 Wb m-2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A paramagnetic material shows a net magnetization of 8 A m-1 when placed in an external magnetic field of intensity  0.6 T  at a temperature of 4 K . When the same material is placed in an external magnetic field of intensity  0.2 T  at a temperature of  16 K , the magnetization will be
questions.solution:
According to Curie's law,

Magnetization, I∝Magnetic Induction(B)Absolute temperature(T)

⇒I2I1=B2T1B1T2

⇒I28=0.2×40.6×16

⇒I2=23 A m-1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A magnetic dipole is placed at right angles to the direction of magnetic induction <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math>. If it is rotated through an angle of <math><mn>180</mn><mo>°</mo></math>, then the work done is
questions.solution:
Work done in rotating a dipole

W=MB cos⁡θ1-cos⁡θ2

To rotate it from 90° to 0°

θ1=90° θ2=0°

W=MBcos⁡90°-cos⁡0°

W1=MB0-1=-MB

W1=-MB .........(i)

To rotate it from 0° to -90° in same manner.

θ1=0° θ2=-90°

W2=MBcos⁡0°-cos⁡-90°

W2=MB ........(ii)

Total workdone

W=W1+W2

W=-MB+MB

W=0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The frequncy of vibration in a vibration magnetometer of the combination of two bar magnets of magnetic moments $M_1$ and $M_2$ is $6 \mathrm{~Hz}$ when like poles are tied and it is $2 \mathrm{~Hz}$ when the unlike poles are tied together, then the ratio $M_1: M_2$ is
questions.solution:
In the sum and difference method of vibration magnetometer,
$\frac{M_1}{M_2}=\frac{T_2^2+T_1^2}{T_2^2-T_1^2}$
Here,
$T_1=\frac{1}{n_1}=\frac{1}{6} \mathrm{~s}$
$T_2=\frac{1}{n_2}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~s}$
$\frac{M_1}{M_2}=\frac{\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{36}}{\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{36}}+\frac{\frac{9+1}{36}}{\frac{9-1}{36}}$
$=\frac{10}{8}=\frac{5}{4}$
$M_1: M_2=5: 4$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics

questions.solution: Electromagnets are made of soft iron. The soft iron has high retentivity and low corectivity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A deflection magnetometer is adjusted and a magnet of magnetic moment $M$ is placed on it in the usual manner and the observed deflection is $\theta$. The period of oscillation of the needle before settling of the deflection is $T$. When the magnet is removed, the period of oscillation of the needle is $T_0$ before settling to $0^{\circ}-0^{\circ}$. If the earth's induced magnetic field is $B_H$, the relation between $T$ and $T_0$ is
questions.solution:
In deflection magnetometer, field due to magnet $F$ and horizontal component $B_H$ of earth's field are perpendicular to each other.
$\therefore$ Net field is $\sqrt{F^2+B_H^2}$
So the time period
$T=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{1}{M \sqrt{F^2+B_H}}}$
When magnet is removed
$T_0=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{1}{M B_H}}$
Also, $\quad \frac{F}{B_H}=\tan \theta$
Dividing Eqs. (i) by (ii), we get
$\begin{aligned} \frac{T}{T_0} & =\sqrt{\frac{B_H}{\sqrt{F^2+B_H^2}}} \\ & =\sqrt{\frac{B_H}{\sqrt{B_H^2+\tan ^2 \theta+B_H^2}}}=\sqrt{\frac{B_H}{B_H \sqrt{\sec ^2 \theta}}} \\ & =\sqrt{\cos \theta} \\ \Rightarrow \quad T^2 & =T_0^2 \cos \theta\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A bar magnet is $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ long is kept with its north $(N)$-pole pointing north. A neutral point is formed at a distance of $15 \mathrm{~cm}$ from each pole. Given the horizontal component of earth's field is 0.4 Gauss, the pole strength of the magnet is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { Length of magnet }=10 \mathrm{~cm}=10 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}, \\ & \qquad r=15 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$
$O P=\sqrt{225-25}=\sqrt{200} \mathrm{~cm}$ Since, at the neutral point, magnetic field due to the magnet is equal to $B_H$ $\begin{gathered} B_H=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \cdot \frac{M}{\left(O P^2+A O^2\right)^{3 / 2}} \\ 0.4 \times 10^{-4}=10^{-7} \times \frac{M}{\left(200 \times 10^{-4}+25 \times 10^{-4}\right)^{3 / 2}} \\ \frac{0.4 \times 10^{-4}}{10^{-7}} \times\left(225 \times 10^{-4}\right)^{3 / 2}=M \\ 0.4 \times 10^3 \times 10^{-6}(225)^{3 / 2}=M \\ M=1.35 \mathrm{~A}-\mathrm{m} \end{gathered}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A deflection magnetometer is placed with its arm along the east-west direction (<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> position) and a short bar magnet is placed symmetrically along its axis at some distance with its north pole pointing towards east. In this position the needle of the magnetometer shows a deflection of <math><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math>. If we double the distance of the bar magnet, then the deflection will be
questions.solution:
For a short bar magnet in tan A position,

μ04π2Md3=H tanθ             ...(i)

When distance is doubled, then new deflection θ′ is given by,

μ04π2M2d3=H tanθ' ....(ii)

∴    tan ⁡ θ ′ tan ⁡θ=18

⇒        tan θ′=tan θ8=tan ⁡60°8=38

⇒        θ′=tan-1⁡38
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A bar magnet of magnetic moment <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is aligned with the direction of a uniform magnetic field of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>22</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>. The work done in turning the magnet so as to align its magnetic moment opposite to the field and the torque acting on it in this position are respectively.
questions.solution:
Potential energy of a magnetic dipole is given as

U = -MB cosθ where, M= Magnetic dipole, B= Magnetic field, θ = Angle between M→ and B→

W= Uf-Ui ⇒W = -MB cosθf - -MB cosθi⇒W = MB cosθi- cosθf⇒W = 1.5×0.22 cos0°-cos180°⇒W = 0.66 J

∵ τ→ = MB sinθ

Given θ= 180°

∴ τ→ = MB sin180°⇒ τ→ = 0
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The deflections produced by a Tangent Galvanometer at two different places when the same current is passed through it are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> respectively. The ratio of the values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></msub></math> at two places is
questions.solution:
Tangent galvanometer is a galvanometer with a very small magnetic needle in the middle of a big vertical circular coil of wire through which electric current is conducted and whose plane is in the magnetic meridian, with the current intensity proportional to the tangent of the needle's angle of deflection.

According to tangent law, the magnetic field when the deflection in needle is θ,

B=BHtanθ

Here, B=μ0nI2r

As, the same current is passed through the galvanometer at both the places, BH∝1tanθ

⇒ BH1BH2=tanθ2tanθ1=tan45°tan60°=31
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The intensity of the magnetic induction field at the centre of a single turn circular coil of radius $5 \mathrm{~cm}$ carrying current of $0.9 \mathrm{~A}$.
questions.solution:
The intensity of magnetic induction field
$B=\frac{\mu_0 i}{2 r}$
$=\frac{4 \pi \times 10^{-7} \times 0.9}{2 \times 5 \times 10^{-2}}$
$B=36 \pi \times 10^{-7} \mathrm{~T}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A bar magnet of magnetic moment M is placed at a distance D with its axis along positive X-axis. Likewise, second bar magnet of magnetic moment M is placed at a distance 2D on positive Y-axis and perpendicular to it as shown in the figure. The magnitude of magnetic field at the origin is B→=αμ04πMD3. The value of α must be (Assume D>> where l is the length of magnets)
questions.solution:
Baxis=2μ0M4πD3 & Bequator=μ0M8πD3

since d=2D

Bnet=Baxis-Bequator=μ0M4πD32-18

∴  Bnet=158μ0M4πD3⇒α=158

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In the hysteresis cycle, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>H</mi></math> needed to make the intensity of magnetization zero is called
questions.solution: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>H</mi></math> needed to make intensity of magnetization zero or to remove the residual magnetism is coercive force or coercivity

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two bar magnets ' $\mathrm{P}$ ' and ' $\mathrm{Q}$ ' are kept in uniform magnetic field 'B' with magnetic moments ' $\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{P}}$ ' and ' $\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{Q}}$ ' respectively. Magnet ' $\mathrm{P}$ ' is oscillating with frequency twice that of magnet ' $\mathrm{Q}$ '. If the moment of inertia of the magnet ' $\mathrm{P}$ ' is twice that of magnet ' $\mathrm{Q}$ ' then
questions.solution:
The frequency of oscillation given by
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{p}}=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{p}} \mathrm{B}}{\mathrm{I}_P}} \text { and } \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{Q}}=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{Q}} \mathrm{B}}{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{Q}}}} \\
& \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{p}}=2 \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{Q}} \\
& \frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{P}} \mathrm{B}}{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{P}}}}=2 \cdot \frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{Q}} \mathrm{B}}{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{Q}}}} \\
& \frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{P}}}{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{P}}}=4 \frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{Q}}}{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{Q}}} \\
& \therefore \frac{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{P}}}{\mathrm{M}_{\mathrm{Q}}}=4 \frac{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{P}}}{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{Q}}}=8 \quad\left[\because \mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{P}}=2 \mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{Q}}\right]
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two short bar magnets of dipole moments M and M3 are joined at right angles to form a cross as depicted in the figure. The value of θ for which the system remains in equilibrium in a uniform external magnetic field B is
questions.solution:
Torque τ acting on magnet (1),

τ1=MBsin⁡θ

Similarly, τ2=3 MBsin⁡θ

But for equilibrium, τ1=τ2

∴   MBsin⁡θ= 3 MBcos⁡θ 

tan⁡θ=3 =tan⁡60°

∴   θ=60°
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: There are <math><mn>2.0</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>24</mn></mrow></msup></math> molecular dipoles in a paramagnetic salt. Each has dipole moment <math><mn>1.5</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mrow><mn>10</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>23</mn></mrow></msup><mi>A</mi><msup><mrow><mi>m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> . Find maximum (saturation) magnetization in the specimen.
questions.solution:
Maximum magnetization

M=μ×no. of atomsunit volume

=1.5×10-23Am2×2×1024

=30 Am2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: At a certain place a magnet makes 30 oscillations per minute. At another place where the magnetic field is double, its time period will be
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} T=2 \pi & \sqrt{\frac{I}{M B_H}} \\ \therefore \quad \frac{T_1}{T_2} & =\sqrt{\frac{\left(B_H\right)_2}{\left(B_H\right)_1}} \\ T_2 & =T_1 \sqrt{\frac{\left(B_H\right)_1}{\left(B_H\right)_2}} \\ n_1 & =30 \text { oscillation } / \mathrm{min} \\ n_1 & =\frac{1}{2} \text { oscillation } / \mathrm{s} \\ T_1 & =2 \mathrm{~s} \\ T_2 & =2 \sqrt{\frac{B_H}{2 B_H}} \\ = & 2 \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~s}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Two short bar magnets have their magnetic moments $1.2 \mathrm{Am}^2$ and $1.0 \mathrm{Am}^2$. They are placed on a horizontal table parallel to each other at a distance of $20 \mathrm{~cm}$ between their centres, such that their north poles pointing towards geographic south. They have common magnetic equatorial line. Horizontal component of earth's field is $3.6 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~T}$. Then, the resultant horizontal magnetic induction at mid point of the line joining their centers is $\left(\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi}=10^{-7} \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}\right)$
questions.solution:
We knows, $B=\frac{\mu_0}{4 \pi} \frac{M}{r^3}$
Hence the resultant horizontal magnetic induction point of the line joining their conters is
$\begin{aligned}
B & =B_1+B_2+B_H \\
& =\frac{10^{-7} \times 1.2}{\left(10 \times 10^{-2}\right)^3}+\frac{10^{-7} \times 1}{\left(10 \times 10^{-2}\right)^3}+3.6 \times 10^{-5} \\
& =1.2 \times 10^{-4}+1 \times 10^{-4}+0.36 \times 10^{-4} \\
& =2.56 \times 10^{-4} \mathrm{~T}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The coil of an a.c. generator has 100 turns, each of cross-sectional area $2 \mathrm{~m}^2$. It is rotating at constant angular speed $30 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$, in a uniform magnetic field of $2 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~T}$. If the total resistance of the circuit is $600 \Omega$ then maximum power dissipated in the circuit is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{N}=100, \mathrm{~A}=2 \mathrm{~m}^2, \omega=30 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \mathrm{B}=2 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~T}, \mathrm{R}=600 \Omega
\end{aligned}$
Maximum power dissipated in the circuit
$\begin{aligned}
P_{\max }=E_{r m s} \times I_{r m s} & =\frac{E_0}{\sqrt{2}} \times \frac{I_0}{\sqrt{2}} \\
& =\frac{E_0 I_0}{2}...(i)
\end{aligned}$
But $\mathrm{I}_0=\frac{\mathrm{E}_0}{\mathrm{R}}...(ii)$
Putting (2) into (1) we get,
$\mathrm{I}_0=\frac{\mathrm{E}_0{ }^2}{2 \mathrm{R}}$
$\begin{aligned}
\text { But } \mathrm{E}_0 & =\mathrm{NAB} \omega \\
\mathrm{E}_0 & =100 \times 2 \times 2 \times 10^{-2} \times 30 \\
& =120 \mathrm{~V} \\
\therefore \quad \mathrm{P}_{\max }= & \frac{120 \times 120}{2 \times 600}=12 \mathrm{~W}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
